# uuid

```go
import "github.com/google/uuid"
```

Package uuid generates and inspects UUIDs.

UUIDs are based on RFC 9562\(obsoletes RFC 4122\) and DCE 1.1: Authentication and Security Services.

A UUID is a 16 byte \(128 bit\) array. UUIDs may be used as keys to maps or compared directly.

## Constants

<a name="Person"></a>Domain constants for DCE Security \(Version 2\) UUIDs.

```go
const (
    Person = Domain(0)
    Group  = Domain(1)
    Org    = Domain(2)
)
```

<a name="Invalid"></a>Constants returned by Variant.

```go
const (
    Invalid   = Variant(iota) // Invalid UUID
    RFC4122                   // The variant specified in RFC9562(obsoletes RFC4122).
    Reserved                  // Reserved, NCS backward compatibility.
    Microsoft                 // Reserved, Microsoft Corporation backward compatibility.
    Future                    // Reserved for future definition.
)
```

<a name="Standard"></a>RFC9562 added V6 and V7 of UUID, but did not change specification of V1 and V4 implemented in this module. To avoid creating new major module version, we still use RFC4122 for constant name.

```go
const Standard = RFC4122
```

## Variables

<a name="NameSpaceDNS"></a>Well known namespace IDs and UUIDs

```go
var (
    NameSpaceDNS  = MustParse("6ba7b810-9dad-11d1-80b4-00c04fd430c8")
    NameSpaceURL  = MustParse("6ba7b811-9dad-11d1-80b4-00c04fd430c8")
    NameSpaceOID  = MustParse("6ba7b812-9dad-11d1-80b4-00c04fd430c8")
    NameSpaceX500 = MustParse("6ba7b814-9dad-11d1-80b4-00c04fd430c8")
    Nil           UUID // empty UUID, all zeros

    // The Max UUID is special form of UUID that is specified to have all 128 bits set to 1.
    Max = UUID{
        0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF,
        0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF,
    }
)
```

```go
var (
    ErrInvalidUUIDFormat      = errors.New("invalid UUID format")
    ErrInvalidBracketedFormat = errors.New("invalid bracketed UUID format")
)
```


<a name="ClockSequence"></a>

## func [ClockSequence](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/time.go#L81>)

```go
func ClockSequence() int
```

ClockSequence returns the current clock sequence, generating one if not already set. The clock sequence is only used for Version 1 UUIDs.

The uuid package does not use global static storage for the clock sequence or the last time a UUID was generated. Unless SetClockSequence is used, a new random clock sequence is generated the first time a clock sequence is requested by ClockSequence, GetTime, or NewUUID. \(section 4.2.1.1\)

<a name="Compare"></a>

## func [Compare](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/util.go#L47>)

```go
func Compare(a, b UUID) int
```

Compare returns an integer comparing two uuids lexicographically. The result will be 0 if a == b, \-1 if a \< b, and \+1 if a \> b.

<a name="DisableRandPool"></a>

## func [DisableRandPool](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/uuid.go#L376>)

```go
func DisableRandPool()
```

DisableRandPool disables the randomness pool if it was previously enabled with EnableRandPool.

Both EnableRandPool and DisableRandPool are not thread\-safe and should only be called when there is no possibility that New or any other UUID Version 4 generation function will be called concurrently.

<a name="EnableRandPool"></a>

## func [EnableRandPool](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/uuid.go#L366>)

```go
func EnableRandPool()
```

EnableRandPool enables internal randomness pool used for Random \(Version 4\) UUID generation. The pool contains random bytes read from the random number generator on demand in batches. Enabling the pool may improve the UUID generation throughput significantly.

Since the pool is stored on the Go heap, this feature may be a bad fit for security sensitive applications.

Both EnableRandPool and DisableRandPool are not thread\-safe and should only be called when there is no possibility that New or any other UUID Version 4 generation function will be called concurrently.

```go
func IsInvalidLengthError(err error) bool
```

IsInvalidLengthError is matcher function for custom error invalidLengthError

<a name="NewString"></a>

## func [NewString](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/version4.go#L21>)

```go
func NewString() string
```

NewString creates a new random UUID and returns it as a string or panics. NewString is equivalent to the expression

```
uuid.New().String()
```

<a name="NodeID"></a>

## func [NodeID](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/node.go#L60>)

```go
func NodeID() []byte
```

NodeID returns a slice of a copy of the current Node ID, setting the Node ID if not already set.

<a name="NodeInterface"></a>

## func [NodeInterface](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/node.go#L21>)

```go
func NodeInterface() string
```

NodeInterface returns the name of the interface from which the NodeID was derived. The interface "user" is returned if the NodeID was set by SetNodeID.

<a name="SetClockSequence"></a>

## func [SetClockSequence](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/time.go#L96>)

```go
func SetClockSequence(seq int)
```

SetClockSequence sets the clock sequence to the lower 14 bits of seq. Setting to \-1 causes a new sequence to be generated.

<a name="SetNodeID"></a>

## func [SetNodeID](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/node.go#L73>)

```go
func SetNodeID(id []byte) bool
```

SetNodeID sets the Node ID to be used for Version 1 UUIDs. The first 6 bytes of id are used. If id is less than 6 bytes then false is returned and the Node ID is not set.

<a name="SetNodeInterface"></a>

## func [SetNodeInterface](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/node.go#L33>)

```go
func SetNodeInterface(name string) bool
```

SetNodeInterface selects the hardware address to be used for Version 1 UUIDs. If name is "" then the first usable interface found will be used or a random Node ID will be generated. If a named interface cannot be found then false is returned.

SetNodeInterface never fails when name is "".

<a name="SetRand"></a>

## func [SetRand](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/uuid.go#L347>)

```go
func SetRand(r io.Reader)
```

SetRand sets the random number generator to r, which implements io.Reader. If r.Read returns an error when the package requests random data then a panic will be issued.

Calling SetRand with nil sets the random number generator to the default generator.

<a name="Validate"></a>

## func [Validate](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/uuid.go#L223>)

```go
func Validate(s string) error
```

Validate returns an error if s is not a properly formatted UUID in one of the following formats:

```
xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx
urn:uuid:xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx
xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
{xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx}
```

It returns an error if the format is invalid, otherwise nil.

<a name="NullUUID"></a>

## type [NullUUID](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/null.go#L29-L32>)

NullUUID represents a UUID that may be null. NullUUID implements the SQL driver.Scanner interface so it can be used as a scan destination:

```
var u uuid.NullUUID
err := db.QueryRow("SELECT name FROM foo WHERE id=?", id).Scan(&u)
...
if u.Valid {
   // use u.UUID
} else {
   // NULL value
}
```

```go
type NullUUID struct {
    UUID  UUID
    Valid bool // Valid is true if UUID is not NULL
}
```

<a name="Time"></a>

## type [Time](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/time.go#L15>)

A Time represents a time as the number of 100's of nanoseconds since 15 Oct 1582.

```go
type Time int64
```

<a name="GetTime"></a>

### func [GetTime](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/time.go#L45>)

```go
func GetTime() (Time, uint16, error)
```

GetTime returns the current Time \(100s of nanoseconds since 15 Oct 1582\) and clock sequence as well as adjusting the clock sequence as needed. An error is returned if the current time cannot be determined.

<a name="Time.UnixTime"></a>

### func \(Time\) [UnixTime](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/time.go#L35>)

```go
func (t Time) UnixTime() (sec, nsec int64)
```

UnixTime converts t the number of seconds and nanoseconds using the Unix epoch of 1 Jan 1970.

<a name="UUID"></a>

## type [UUID](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/uuid.go#L20>)

A UUID is a 128 bit \(16 byte\) Universal Unique IDentifier as defined in RFC 9562.

```go
type UUID [16]byte
```

<a name="FromBytes"></a>

### func [FromBytes](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/uuid.go#L204>)

```go
func FromBytes(b []byte) (uuid UUID, err error)
```

FromBytes creates a new UUID from a byte slice. Returns an error if the slice does not have a length of 16. The bytes are copied from the slice.

<a name="Must"></a>

### func [Must](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/uuid.go#L210>)

```go
func Must(uuid UUID, err error) UUID
```

Must returns uuid if err is nil and panics otherwise.

<a name="MustParse"></a>

### func [MustParse](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/uuid.go#L194>)

```go
func MustParse(s string) UUID
```

MustParse is like Parse but panics if the string cannot be parsed. It simplifies safe initialization of global variables holding compiled UUIDs.

<a name="New"></a>

### func [New](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/version4.go#L13>)

```go
func New() UUID
```

New creates a new random UUID or panics. New is equivalent to the expression

```
uuid.Must(uuid.NewRandom())
```

<a name="NewDCEGroup"></a>

### func [NewDCEGroup](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/dce.go#L54>)

```go
func NewDCEGroup() (UUID, error)
```

NewDCEGroup returns a DCE Security \(Version 2\) UUID in the group domain with the id returned by os.Getgid.

```
NewDCESecurity(Group, uint32(os.Getgid()))
```

<a name="NewDCEPerson"></a>

### func [NewDCEPerson](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/dce.go#L46>)

```go
func NewDCEPerson() (UUID, error)
```

NewDCEPerson returns a DCE Security \(Version 2\) UUID in the person domain with the id returned by os.Getuid.

```
NewDCESecurity(Person, uint32(os.Getuid()))
```

<a name="NewDCESecurity"></a>

### func [NewDCESecurity](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/dce.go#L32>)

```go
func NewDCESecurity(domain Domain, id uint32) (UUID, error)
```

NewDCESecurity returns a DCE Security \(Version 2\) UUID.

The domain should be one of Person, Group or Org. On a POSIX system the id should be the users UID for the Person domain and the users GID for the Group. The meaning of id for the domain Org or on non\-POSIX systems is site defined.

For a given domain/id pair the same token may be returned for up to 7 minutes and 10 seconds.

<a name="NewHash"></a>

### func [NewHash](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/hash.go#L33>)

```go
func NewHash(h hash.Hash, space UUID, data []byte, version int) UUID
```

NewHash returns a new UUID derived from the hash of space concatenated with data generated by h. The hash should be at least 16 byte in length. The first 16 bytes of the hash are used to form the UUID. The version of the UUID will be the lower 4 bits of version. NewHash is used to implement NewMD5 and NewSHA1.

<a name="NewMD5"></a>

### func [NewMD5](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/hash.go#L49>)

```go
func NewMD5(space UUID, data []byte) UUID
```

NewMD5 returns a new MD5 \(Version 3\) UUID based on the supplied name space and data. It is the same as calling:

```
NewHash(md5.New(), space, data, 3)
```

<a name="NewRandom"></a>

### func [NewRandom](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/version4.go#L39>)

```go
func NewRandom() (UUID, error)
```

NewRandom returns a Random \(Version 4\) UUID.

The strength of the UUIDs is based on the strength of the crypto/rand package.

Uses the randomness pool if it was enabled with EnableRandPool.

A note about uniqueness derived from the UUID Wikipedia entry:

```
Randomly generated UUIDs have 122 random bits.  One's annual risk of being
hit by a meteorite is estimated to be one chance in 17 billion, that
means the probability is about 0.00000000006 (6 × 10−11),
equivalent to the odds of creating a few tens of trillions of UUIDs in a
year and having one duplicate.
```

<a name="NewRandomFromReader"></a>

### func [NewRandomFromReader](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/version4.go#L47>)

```go
func NewRandomFromReader(r io.Reader) (UUID, error)
```

NewRandomFromReader returns a UUID based on bytes read from a given io.Reader.

<a name="NewSHA1"></a>

### func [NewSHA1](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/hash.go#L57>)

```go
func NewSHA1(space UUID, data []byte) UUID
```

NewSHA1 returns a new SHA1 \(Version 5\) UUID based on the supplied name space and data. It is the same as calling:

```
NewHash(sha1.New(), space, data, 5)
```

<a name="NewUUID"></a>

### func [NewUUID](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/version1.go#L19>)

```go
func NewUUID() (UUID, error)
```

NewUUID returns a Version 1 UUID based on the current NodeID and clock sequence, and the current time. If the NodeID has not been set by SetNodeID or SetNodeInterface then it will be set automatically. If the NodeID cannot be set NewUUID returns nil. If clock sequence has not been set by SetClockSequence then it will be set automatically. If GetTime fails to return the current NewUUID returns nil and an error.

In most cases, New should be used.

<a name="NewV6"></a>

### func [NewV6](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/version6.go#L24>)

```go
func NewV6() (UUID, error)
```

UUID version 6 is a field\-compatible version of UUIDv1, reordered for improved DB locality. It is expected that UUIDv6 will primarily be used in contexts where there are existing v1 UUIDs. Systems that do not involve legacy UUIDv1 SHOULD consider using UUIDv7 instead.

see https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9562#uuidv6

NewV6 returns a Version 6 UUID based on the current NodeID and clock sequence, and the current time. If the NodeID has not been set by SetNodeID or SetNodeInterface then it will be set automatically. If the NodeID cannot be set NewV6 set NodeID is random bits automatically . If clock sequence has not been set by SetClockSequence then it will be set automatically. If GetTime fails to return the current NewV6 returns Nil and an error.

<a name="NewV6WithTime"></a>

### func [NewV6WithTime](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/version6.go#L39>)

```go
func NewV6WithTime(customTime *time.Time) (UUID, error)
```

NewV6WithTime returns a Version 6 UUID based on the current NodeID, clock sequence, and a specified time. It is similar to the NewV6 function, but allows you to specify the time. If time is passed as nil, then the current time is used.

There is a limit on how many UUIDs can be generated for the same time, so if you are generating multiple UUIDs, it is recommended to increment the time. If getTime fails to return the current NewV6WithTime returns Nil and an error.

<a name="NewV7"></a>

### func [NewV7](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/version7.go#L23>)

```go
func NewV7() (UUID, error)
```

UUID version 7 features a time\-ordered value field derived from the widely implemented and well known Unix Epoch timestamp source, the number of milliseconds seconds since midnight 1 Jan 1970 UTC, leap seconds excluded. As well as improved entropy characteristics over versions 1 or 6.

see https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9562#name-uuid-version-7

Implementations SHOULD utilize UUID version 7 over UUID version 1 and 6 if possible.

NewV7 returns a Version 7 UUID based on the current time\(Unix Epoch\). Uses the randomness pool if it was enabled with EnableRandPool. On error, NewV7 returns Nil and an error

<a name="NewV7FromReader"></a>

### func [NewV7FromReader](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/version7.go#L35>)

```go
func NewV7FromReader(r io.Reader) (UUID, error)
```

NewV7FromReader returns a Version 7 UUID based on the current time\(Unix Epoch\). it use NewRandomFromReader fill random bits. On error, NewV7FromReader returns Nil and an error.

<a name="Parse"></a>

### func [Parse](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/uuid.go#L95>)

```go
func Parse(s string) (UUID, error)
```

Parse decodes s into a UUID or returns an error if it cannot be parsed. Both the standard UUID forms defined in RFC 9562 \(xxxxxxxx\-xxxx\-xxxx\-xxxx\-xxxxxxxxxxxx and urn:uuid:xxxxxxxx\-xxxx\-xxxx\-xxxx\-xxxxxxxxxxxx\) are decoded. In addition, Parse accepts non\-standard strings such as the raw hex encoding xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx and 38 byte "Microsoft style" encodings, e.g. \{xxxxxxxx\-xxxx\-xxxx\-xxxx\-xxxxxxxxxxxx\}. Only the middle 36 bytes are examined in the latter case. Parse should not be used to validate strings as it parses non\-standard encodings as indicated above.

<a name="ParseBytes"></a>

### func [ParseBytes](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/uuid.go#L148>)

```go
func ParseBytes(b []byte) (UUID, error)
```

ParseBytes is like Parse, except it parses a byte slice instead of a string.

<a name="UUID.ClockSequence"></a>

### func \(UUID\) [ClockSequence](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/time.go#L139>)

```go
func (uuid UUID) ClockSequence() int
```

ClockSequence returns the clock sequence encoded in uuid. The clock sequence is only well defined for version 1 and 2 UUIDs.

<a name="UUID.Domain"></a>

### func \(UUID\) [Domain](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/dce.go#L60>)

```go
func (uuid UUID) Domain() Domain
```

Domain returns the domain for a Version 2 UUID. Domains are only defined for Version 2 UUIDs.

<a name="UUID.ID"></a>

### func \(UUID\) [ID](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/dce.go#L66>)

```go
func (uuid UUID) ID() uint32
```

ID returns the id for a Version 2 UUID. IDs are only defined for Version 2 UUIDs.

<a name="UUID.MarshalBinary"></a>

### func \(UUID\) [MarshalBinary](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/marshal.go#L27>)

```go
func (uuid UUID) MarshalBinary() ([]byte, error)
```

MarshalBinary implements encoding.BinaryMarshaler.

<a name="UUID.MarshalText"></a>

### func \(UUID\) [MarshalText](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/marshal.go#L10>)

```go
func (uuid UUID) MarshalText() ([]byte, error)
```

MarshalText implements encoding.TextMarshaler.

<a name="UUID.NodeID"></a>

### func \(UUID\) [NodeID](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/node.go#L86>)

```go
func (uuid UUID) NodeID() []byte
```

NodeID returns the 6 byte node id encoded in uuid. It returns nil if uuid is not valid. The NodeID is only well defined for version 1 and 2 UUIDs.

<a name="UUID.Scan"></a>

### func \(\*UUID\) [Scan](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/sql.go#L15>)

```go
func (uuid *UUID) Scan(src interface{}) error
```

Scan implements sql.Scanner so UUIDs can be read from databases transparently. Currently, database types that map to string and \[\]byte are supported. Please consult database\-specific driver documentation for matching types.

<a name="UUID.String"></a>

### func \(UUID\) [String](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/uuid.go#L272>)

```go
func (uuid UUID) String() string
```

String returns the string form of uuid, xxxxxxxx\-xxxx\-xxxx\-xxxx\-xxxxxxxxxxxx , or "" if uuid is invalid.

<a name="UUID.Time"></a>

### func \(UUID\) [Time](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/time.go#L117>)

```go
func (uuid UUID) Time() Time
```

Time returns the time in 100s of nanoseconds since 15 Oct 1582 encoded in uuid. The time is only defined for version 1, 2, 6 and 7 UUIDs.

<a name="UUID.URN"></a>

### func \(UUID\) [URN](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/uuid.go#L280>)

```go
func (uuid UUID) URN() string
```

URN returns the RFC 2141 URN form of uuid, urn:uuid:xxxxxxxx\-xxxx\-xxxx\-xxxx\-xxxxxxxxxxxx, or "" if uuid is invalid.

<a name="UUID.UnmarshalBinary"></a>

### func \(\*UUID\) [UnmarshalBinary](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/marshal.go#L32>)

```go
func (uuid *UUID) UnmarshalBinary(data []byte) error
```

UnmarshalBinary implements encoding.BinaryUnmarshaler.

<a name="UUID.UnmarshalText"></a>

### func \(\*UUID\) [UnmarshalText](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/marshal.go#L17>)

```go
func (uuid *UUID) UnmarshalText(data []byte) error
```

UnmarshalText implements encoding.TextUnmarshaler.

<a name="UUID.Value"></a>

### func \(UUID\) [Value](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/sql.go#L57>)

```go
func (uuid UUID) Value() (driver.Value, error)
```

Value implements sql.Valuer so that UUIDs can be written to databases transparently. Currently, UUIDs map to strings. Please consult database\-specific driver documentation for matching types.

<a name="UUID.Variant"></a>

### func \(UUID\) [Variant](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/uuid.go#L300>)

```go
func (uuid UUID) Variant() Variant
```

Variant returns the variant encoded in uuid.

<a name="UUID.Version"></a>

### func \(UUID\) [Version](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/uuid.go#L314>)

```go
func (uuid UUID) Version() Version
```

Version returns the version of uuid.

<a name="UUIDs"></a>

## type [UUIDs](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/uuid.go#L384>)

UUIDs is a slice of UUID types.

```go
type UUIDs []UUID
```

<a name="UUIDs.Strings"></a>

### func \(UUIDs\) [Strings](<https://github.com/google/uuid/blob/master/uuid.go#L387>)

```go
func (uuids UUIDs) Strings() []string
```

Strings returns a string slice containing the string form of each UUID in uuids.

